hints.text: Unique car on ALL coverage: **two metal bars w/ thick black stripes** + white hood visible at the front
hints.uniq_note: One Mongolian car has similar stripes (landscape separates); Guatemala/Curaçao have metal bars WITHOUT thick black stripes.

shared_with.note: A Mongolian car has similar striped bars.
Metal bars, but no thick black stripes; mirrors visible.

hints.text: Poles mostly **square concrete w/ a reverse-'L' top** (round/octagonal rarer)

shared_with.note: The only other square-pole country in the Americas.

hints.text: Plates **pale yellow top / white bottom** (New-Jersey-like); front plates not required (in LatAm otherwise only PR + Panama)
Roads often of **smooth, noticeably darker asphalt**
Stop signs say **'PARE'** despite the Central American location; many signs on **yellow signposts**
Coverage very limited: **only Santo Domingo + Santiago de los Caballeros**

**Mountains to the north = always Santiago de los Caballeros** (Santo Domingo has hills, never real mountains); Santiago also has **yellow traffic-light poles** (yellow signposts exist in both cities!)

**Santo Domingo**: developed downtown w/ tall buildings + wide avenues; street signs w/ **'ADN'** (Distrito Nacional); **black-purple pole markings** only here; outskirts = whitish dirt tracks, dry shrubs, debris
Some **late-evening coverage in Santo Domingo** — Santiago has none
